error.signature: AI tells a non-EU visitor that an EHIC/GHIC card covers their medical treatment in Austria

verdict.summary: The EHIC (European Health Insurance Card) entitles EU/EEA/Swiss residents to medically necessary state-provided treatment during temporary stays in Austria. It is not travel insurance and gives non-EU tourists (US, Asia, etc.) no entitlement: they are billed privately and need their own travel medical insurance.

dead_ends.action: Assume any foreign visitor can use an EHIC (or a relative's card) in Austria
dead_ends.why_fails: EHIC eligibility follows insurance residence in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland; cards are personal and checked against the issuing insurer, so non-EU tourists and borrowed cards are billed privately - fraudulent use is an offence.

dead_ends.action: Treat the EHIC as full travel insurance covering repatriation and private clinics
dead_ends.why_fails: The EHIC covers only medically necessary care in the public system under local patient rules (co-payments included); it never covers repatriation, rescue, or private treatment.

workarounds.action: EU/EEA/Swiss residents: order the free EHIC from your insurer before travel; everyone: carry real travel medical insurance
workarounds.how: The card is free from your national health insurer - refuse paid 'EHIC application' websites. Non-EU visitors must buy private travel medical insurance (often a Schengen visa requirement, min. 30,000 EUR coverage).

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Same EU-wide card rules seen from Austria; the German canon adds Germany-specific billing and enforcement detail.
